subjects.name: Reasoning Ability (Prelims & Mains)
subjects.analysis: High level of abstraction. Prelims focuses on speed, while Mains introduces complex data-driven puzzles.

subjects.name: Quantitative Aptitude / Data Interpretation
subjects.analysis: LIC Prelims involves speed math; Mains is almost entirely Data Interpretation and Analysis.

subjects.name: Insurance & Financial Market Awareness (Mains)
subjects.analysis: The most critical section for LIC AAO. Covers the history of insurance, IRDAI regulations, and current financial trends.

subjects.name: General Knowledge & Current Affairs (Mains)
subjects.analysis: Broad focus on national and international events. 6-8 months of coverage recommended.

subjects.name: English Language & Descriptive
subjects.analysis: Prelims English is qualifying only (Marks not added to total). Mains Descriptive is mandatory.

mentorship_data.title: Prelims Strategy: The Qualifying Hurdle
mentorship_data.content: English marks are NOT added to your score. Focus 100% on Reasoning and Quant to maximize your total out of 70 marks.

mentorship_data.title: Mains Strategy: Specialized Focus
mentorship_data.content: The Insurance Awareness section is the game-changer. Unlike Banking GA, this requires technical knowledge of insurance principles and IRDAI guidelines.

mentorship_data.title: Negative Marking Caution
mentorship_data.content: With a 0.25 penalty, blind guessing in the Insurance section can be fatal. Ensure accuracy in technical terminology.

mentorship_data.title: Interview & Documentation
mentorship_data.content: LIC looks for professional ethics and communication. Be prepared to discuss current LIC policies and why you want to join the insurance sector.

frequently_asked_questions.q: Are English marks added to the final merit?
frequently_asked_questions.a: No, in Prelims, the English section is only for qualifying purposes. Your merit rank is decided based on Reasoning and Quant in Prelims, and the Mains score.

frequently_asked_questions.q: Is there a negative marking in LIC AAO?
frequently_asked_questions.a: Yes, there is a negative marking of 0.25 marks for every incorrect answer in both Prelims and Mains.

frequently_asked_questions.q: What is the minimum qualifying mark for the Interview?
frequently_asked_questions.a: Generally, it is 30 marks for Unreserved and 27 for SC/ST, but it varies by the official notification.

frequently_asked_questions.q: What is the service bond for LIC AAO?
frequently_asked_questions.a: Candidates must serve for a minimum of 4 years (including probation). Failure to do so results in a penalty (approx. ₹5,00,000).
The probation period is 1 year, which can be extended up to 2 years based on performance.

frequently_asked_questions.q: Can I apply for multiple specialist posts?
frequently_asked_questions.a: No, a candidate can apply for only one post (Generalist or a specific Specialist post).
